keyPoints: In an infinitely repeated Prisoner's dilemma, the Nash equilibrium involves cooperative behavior to deter defection
If players cooperate, they both receive a higher payoff than if they defect
However, if one player defects while the other cooperates, the defector receives a higher payoff and the cooperator receives a lower payoff
Applying the Nash equilibrium concept, both players choose to cooperate in the long run to maximize their payoffs
This is because the repeated interactions allow each player to adjust their strategy to maximize their expected payoff
